records.ety: [OE. cloer to nail up, F. clouer, fr. OF. clo nail, F. clou, fr. L. clavus nail. Cf. 3d Clove.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Cloyed (kloid); p. pr. & vb. n. Cloying.]

defs: To fill or choke up; to stop up; to clog.
quotes: The duke's purpose was to have cloyed the harbor by sinking ships, laden with stones.

defs: To glut, or satisfy, as the appetite; to satiate; to fill to loathing; to surfeit.
quotes: [Who can] cloy the hungry edge of appetite By bare imagination of a feast?
He sometimes cloys his readers instead of satisfying.

defs: To penetrate or pierce; to wound.
quotes: Which, with his cruel tusk, him deadly cloyed.
He never shod horse but he cloyed him.
